companyNameJapanese: ルネサスエレクトロニクス株式会社
description: Japan's largest and world's leading automotive semiconductor company, formed 2010 from NEC Electronics + Renesas Technology (Hitachi/Mitsubishi JV). Global leader in automotive MCUs (over 40% market share in 32-bit automotive MCUs), with a broad portfolio covering automotive SoCs, ADAS processors, power management ICs, and analog/mixed-signal chips. Also serves industrial, IoT, and infrastructure markets. Key product families: RH850 and R-Car automotive MCUs/SoCs, RA/RX/RL78 general microcontrollers, RAA power management ICs. DENSO was a strategic shareholder (~10% stake, gradually reduced), sold ~4%+ in May 2024 and retained ~5%; long-term supply relationship for engine ECU and body electronics MCUs continues. Acquired Intersil (2017), Integrated Device Technology (2019), and Dialog Semiconductor (2021) for broad analog/mixed-signal portfolio expansion.

_externalRelationships.densoRelationship: Long-term automotive MCU supplier to DENSO. DENSO held ~10% strategic stake from 2018 to support automotive SoC co-development; reduced to ~5% in May 2024 via open-market sale (~178B JPY proceeds). Renesas MCUs are used in DENSO engine management ECUs, body electronics, thermal management controllers, and ADAS processing.
keyProducts: RH850 automotive MCU — powertrain, body, chassis ECUs (32-bit, ISO 26262 ASIL-D)
R-Car SoC — automotive infotainment, ADAS processing, cockpit domain controller
RA/RX/RL78 MCUs — general-purpose microcontrollers for IoT and industrial
RAA power management ICs — automotive-grade PMIC
Analog/mixed-signal (from Intersil, IDT, Dialog acquisitions) — wide portfolio
keyCustomers: Bosch (private) — automotive MCUs for European market

_metadata.dataSource: DENSO CMP-0122 deep research (DENSO sold Renesas stake May 2024; retained ~5%; long-term MCU supply), Renesas IR website, StockAnalysis
_metadata.notes: Added as DENSO (CMP-0122) key semiconductor supplier. Market cap approximate. Financial details pending dedicated deep research.
